insight: Going for Arka Sodara forces your opponent to respond immediately or face a significant disadvantage; the second player to commit only benefits if they do so within half a turn (i.e., immediately on their next action).
author: awaclus
context: Discussion about whether Arka Sodara is overpowered and how the 'second player advantage' noted on the wiki actually applies only in a narrow timing window.

insight: Arka Sodara is considered by some high-level players (including apooche) to be the strongest unit in the game, yet it leads to engaging games partly because both players always have symmetric access to it. Several community members do want it nerfed.
author: consensus
context: Extended community debate on whether Arka Sodara should be nerfed, referencing the symmetry of access in Prismata as a mitigating factor.

insight: The wiki's strategic analysis of Arka Sodara's attack-vs-absorb tradeoff assumes the alternative is absorbing on Wall and that both players have perfectly granular defense generation with no overdefense or defender value — assumptions that are not stated in the wiki entry and make the numbers misleading without that context.
author: apooche
context: apooche explained the hidden assumptions behind a wiki passage about Arka Sodara's offensive vs. defensive value, in response to a new player confused by unexplained numbers.

insight: Nitrocybe is useful as a pseudo-Pixie when your attack is non-granular (e.g., three Cynestras and two Tarsiers), because the threat of activating it forces the opponent to always maintain granular defense, even if you never use the ability.
author: xyotsuba
context: Discussion about when Nitrocybe is worth building over Engineers, touching on its role as a flexible threat tool in non-granular attack compositions.

insight: Nitrocybe is ideally timed to come online just as the opponent's threat pressure peaks, after which you transition to Engineers; it is also particularly valuable when you have no granular attack source.
author: xyotsuba
context: amalloy noted he misjudged Nitrocybes as efficient defenders; xyotsuba elaborated on the correct use cases for the unit.

insight: The in-game threat display naively sums all potential enemy damage including from units that cannot all fire simultaneously (e.g., four Drakes with no Blastforges shows 16 damage instead of 8), and similarly adds chill values without accounting for whether the opponent can actually exploit the chill efficiently.
author: awaclus
context: Players discussing how the damage/threat indicator misleads by not accounting for resource constraints like Blastforge supply or efficient chill usage.

insight: Apollo's threat calculation is handled correctly by the in-game display, unlike most other units whose threat is overestimated due to resource constraints not being modeled.
author: f300xen
context: Side remark during discussion about the game's inaccurate threat indicator, noting Apollo as an exception.

insight: Polywall is the best pure soak unit in the game, superior to Aegis in raw soak value; Aegis is better at avoiding exploitation. With Savior specifically, Polywall is preferred over Aegis because Savior benefits more from generating Drones than Conduits.
author: liadahlia
context: Discussion about whether Savior can beat a Venge Cannon all-in, leading to analysis of which soak units pair best with Savior.

insight: A 'naked Tia Threnody' (rushing Tia without red units already in play) almost never works unless the opponent misplays, because you will have committed to the Animus without the economy to support it.
author: consensus
context: Post-game analysis where a player admitted going for an early Tia without having drones or red units ready, confirmed as a throw by multiple participants.

insight: Tia Threnody was previously considered a problematic unit and was subsequently nerfed, which resolved community complaints about it.
author: awaclus
context: Discussion comparing Arka Sodara to past problem units; awaclus confirmed Tia Threnody was nerfed as the reason complaints stopped.

insight: In Venge Cannon all-in matchups, Savior's defensive value depends heavily on having strong soak available; without sufficient soak, Savior loses to a committed Venge Cannon rush.
author: liadahlia
context: Player asked if Savior wins against a Venge Cannon all-in; liadahlia clarified it depends on soak availability.

insight: Top-level bullet (6-second) players can defend a 'goose rush' but defending a 'venge rush' in bullet without Matrix or similar units is considered essentially impossible at any level of play.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of player punf's bullet game performance and what high-level bullet players can and cannot defend against.
